pattern: Turn overlapping range queries into a per-index coverage count via a difference array, then pair the most-covered slots with the largest values.
hints: You get to choose the permutation once, so what matters is only how many requests cover each position, not which requests they are.
Build a difference array: +1 at each request's start, -1 just after its end, then prefix-sum it to get a coverage count per index in O(n + requests).
The best permutation puts the largest values at the most-covered positions: sort the coverage counts and the values, then pair them up largest-with-largest, sum the products modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: single request covering the whole array
single-index requests concentrating weight on one slot
requests spanning every possible sub-range of a small array

stress.description: large random values with many overlapping random ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    r = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    requests = []
    for _ in range(r):
        a = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        b = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if a > b:
            a, b = b, a
        requests.append([a, b])
    return [nums, requests]
stress.note: random start/end pairs normalized so start <= end

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maxSumRangeQuery(self, nums, requests):
        MOD = 10 ** 9 + 7
        n = len(nums)
        diff = [0] * (n + 1)
        for start, end in requests:
            diff[start] += 1
            diff[end + 1] -= 1
        count = [0] * n
        running = 0
        for i in range(n):
            running += diff[i]
            count[i] = running
        count.sort()
        nums_sorted = sorted(nums)
        total = sum(c * v for c, v in zip(count, nums_sorted))
        return total % MOD
